A School of the Human Animal

Movement rooted in martial tradition, somatic wisdom, and the intelligence of the living body.

Explore the Work

What We Teach

Three Paths.
One Body.

01

Rewilding the Human Animal

Movement as instinct. This class strips away structure and returns the body to its natural intelligence — crawling, rolling, leaping, playing. Students learn to follow sensation, curiosity, and intuition as guides, rediscovering the animal expression that our modern life has lost.

02

Body Engineering

The connective tissues of your body — bones, tendons, and muscles — are a system of chains. The way to get a stronger system is to develop the weakest link. Through joint-specific exercises, postural practices, and whole-body calisthenics, we search out and strengthen weak links. This is how we create holistic strength.

03

Systema

The Russian martial art of breath, relaxation, posture, and movement. The goal is not to learn techniques — it is to release unnecessary tension, develop natural strength, and restore confidence so we move through conflict freely and effectively, under a variety of conditions — including striking, grappling, and weapons work.

The Instructor

Aaron Rivera
Dominguez

Systema — Vladimir Vasiliev

Filipino Kali — Shifu Kanishka

Budokon — Cameron Shayne

Black Belt — Age 13

Wilderness Therapy Guide

FRC Mobility Specialist — Functional Anatomy Seminars

Aaron Rivera Dominguez has spent nearly three decades studying the human body in motion — as a martial artist, a movement coach, a wilderness guide, and a somatic practitioner.

He began with Karate at age six, studying form and structure, and earning his black belt at thirteen. Each tradition that followed deepened the inquiry. Krav Maga helped him unleash aggression. Wing Chun showed him the power of sensitivity. At twenty-eight he traveled to India to train with Shifu Kanishka in Filipino Kali and Wing Chun. A year later he began studying Russian Systema under Vladimir Vasiliev — and has since become an instructor-in-training.

Systema gave Aaron a psychosomatic perspective — a perspective he now applies far beyond martial art, weaving the mental, emotional, and relational dimensions of human experience into every class. He has also studied in Cameron Shayne's Budokon Mixed Movement Arts school in Montana, and holds a Mobility Specialist certification through Functional Anatomy Seminars' Functional Range Conditioning program.

Aaron has taught regular classes at yoga studios, led workshops at festivals, and worked with private clients across disciplines. His classes draw from martial arts, functional movement, animal locomotion, and somatic practice — meeting students where they are and leading them to an innate freedom and joy of movement.

In the Field

Budokon Mixed Movement Arts — Montana, 2024

For Studios & Gyms

One Free Class.
No Obligation.

Bringing something new to your community is a risk. Let's remove it. I offer one complimentary class at your location — open to your members, promoted locally, with you in the room. If it lands, we talk. If it doesn't, you've lost nothing.
